title: ruizia ferruginea subsp. borbonica
intro: Ruizia ferruginea subsp. borbonica, also known as the copper leaved Ruizia, is a species of plant within the family Anacardiaceae. It is a succulent shrub native to the island of Réunion, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Ruizia ferruginea subsp. borbonica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.
